summary: Fatima is a 25-year-old seamstress from Dhaka who runs a small tailoring business from home and has trained 6 women in her neighborhood through an informal group. She uses bKash for all transactions.

skills.evidence: Takes customer measurements and cuts patterns
Uses bKash for all business transactions

skills.source_activity: Breaking garment construction into ordered steps
Teaching tailoring to neighborhood women step by step
Understanding customer requests and translating to garment design
Running home-based tailoring business for 4 years
Pricing garments to cover fabric and labor costs
Ensuring students meet quality standards in training
Organizing and leading training group of women

intake_transcript.question: What do you spend most of your time doing each day?
intake_transcript.answer: I sew clothes from home. Customers come with fabric and I make salwar kameez, blouses, school uniforms.

intake_transcript.question: What skills have you learned on your own?
intake_transcript.answer: I learned all my tailoring from my aunt, not from any school. I also taught myself how to use bKash so customers can pay me on their phone.

intake_transcript.question: What languages do you speak?
intake_transcript.answer: Bengali only. A little bit of English words I know from the garment spec sheets my sister shows me.

intake_transcript.question: Have you ever taught someone or organized people?
intake_transcript.answer: Yes, I teach tailoring to women in my neighborhood. Six women are learning from me now in our weekly group.

intake_transcript.question: What tools or technology do you use?
intake_transcript.answer: Sewing machine, scissors, measuring tape, chalk, iron. And bKash on my phone for payments.
